finding: Unfamiliar appearance is treated as infection cue via the same psychological mechanism that categorizes manifestly infected in-group members, such that visibly different out-group members get confused with sick in-group members more often when infection signals are present.

People make Bayesian probability judgments integrating group base rates with observed behavior, yet condemn identical judgments in others and share less money with those who make them, revealing an inconsistency between individual calculation and social evaluation.
Children as young as 4 years store multi-element events as holistic units with correlated retrieval success, but the coherence of this integration increases from age 4 through young adulthood.

Sound-shape matching (bouba-kiki effect) is mediated by arousal elicited by acoustic features, such that higher-arousal sounds are matched with spiky shapes and lower-arousal sounds with rounded shapes.

Patients with generalized anxiety disorder show faster disengagement from threatening facial expressions rather than the expected impaired disengagement, indicating attentional avoidance in clinical anxiety opposite to patterns in subclinical high-trait anxiety.

Combining augmented feedback with external focus of attention produces superior motor performance with reduced muscular activity, suggesting additive benefits from motivation-driven and efficiency-driven mechanisms.
